verses.text: Old David makes Solomon king and numbers the Levites — twenty-four thousand to oversee the work, others as officers, porters, and four thousand musicians. He orders worship for the years to come. There is no retirement from serving God; the aged king prepares for generations he will not see.

David divides the Levites by the families of Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. The work is apportioned by household. God's service is ordered, each family called to its appointed part.

David divides the Levites by the families of Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. The work is apportioned by household. God's service is ordered, each family called to its appointed part.
David divides the Levites by the families of Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. The work is apportioned by household. God's service is ordered, each family called to its appointed part.
The houses of Gershon are reckoned, some counted together for their fewer sons. Even small families have their place. God numbers and assigns the least as carefully as the greatest.
The houses of Gershon are reckoned, some counted together for their fewer sons. Even small families have their place. God numbers and assigns the least as carefully as the greatest.

Kohath's line is traced, with Aaron set apart to sanctify the most holy things forever, and Moses' sons numbered simply among the Levites. The lawgiver sought no dynasty for his children. God's honours fall where He wills, not by human ambition.

The lines of Moses' sons, Gershom and Eliezer, are recorded among the ordinary Levites. The greatest leader's family melts quietly into the ranks. True greatness in God's service does not grasp at privilege for its own.

The descendants of Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel are listed. Branch by branch the tribe is set in order. God's house was served by many families, each given its station.
The descendants of Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel are listed. Branch by branch the tribe is set in order. God's house was served by many families, each given its station.
The descendants of Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel are listed. Branch by branch the tribe is set in order. God's house was served by many families, each given its station.

The sons of Merari are reckoned, noting daughters married within the tribe to keep the inheritance. Even details of marriage and land are preserved. God's care extends to every household of His servants.

By David's last words the Levites are numbered from twenty years old, for the LORD has given rest and they need no longer carry the tabernacle. Worship changes from wandering to settled. God brings His people to rest, and reorders their service for the new day.

Their duty is to assist the priests, care for the holy things, and 'to stand every morning to thank and praise the LORD, and likewise at even.' Daily praise becomes their settled work. The worship of God is to be the steady, morning-and-evening rhythm of His people's life.
